headline: Marian Rivera, Dingdong Dantes, and kids, stun in Dubai portraits  GMA News Online
text:
The Dantes Squad proves yet again that they have that "it" factor!
On Instagram, Marian Rivera shared photos from their Dubai trip, and all of them are looking good.
Marian and Dingdong are ever the picture perfect couple at the desert, while their kids, Zia and Sixto, struck a couple of model-worthy poses for the camera.
"Making memories that will last a lifetime with my favorite people," Marian wrote in the caption.
Earlier, Marian shared photos from their trip to show how different her life has become since she and Dingdong had kids.
More of the family's recent photos show them holding up a majestic owl as well as enjoying the the WB theme park.
Marian and Dingdong celebrated their 10th wedding anniversary in December with a renewal of vows.
Ahead of their milestone occasion, Dingdong posted a few snaps of their prenup shoot taken in Dubai. —JCB, GMA Integrated News

headline: PNP nabs suspect in killing of FEU footballer Keith Absalon  GMA News Online
text:
The National Task Force to End Local Communist Armed Conflict (NTF-ELCAC) announced on Saturday that the Philippine National Police (PNP) has apprehended a suspect in the 2021 killing of footballer Keith Absalon and his cousin Nolven.
In a press release, the NTF-ELCAC commended the recent arrest of alias "Onang," who was also a suspected high-ranking member of the New People’s Army (NPA) and one of the most wanted individuals in the Bicol Region. Onang was apprehended during a police operation in San Fernando, Masbate.
“Today, we are one step closer to justice for Keith, Nolven, and their grieving families,” said NTF-ELCAC Executive Director Undersecretary Ernesto Torres Jr.
The operation was conducted based on arrest warrants issued by the Regional Trial Court (RTC) 5th Judicial Region, Branch Masbate City. Warrants for Onang and 23 others were related to the Absalon case.
Onang faces multiple charges, including violations of the International Humanitarian Law and Other Crimes Against Humanity (RA 9851), under Criminal Case Nos. 21293 and 21294, with bail set at PHP 200,000 each.
Additionally, she is charged with two counts of murder under Criminal Case Nos. 21295 and 21296, for which no bail was recommended, and one count of attempted murder under Criminal Case No. 21297, with bail set at PHP 120,000.
Keith Absalon, 21, and his cousin were victims of an Improvised Explosive Device (IED) blast while riding bicycles with family members in Masbate.
Keith began his football career with Far Eastern University (FEU) in 2013 as a 13-year-old aspirant and became one of the program’s most celebrated high school players.
He earned multiple Midfielder of the Year awards, was named MVP of UAAP Season 78, and became a six-time UAAP champion in high school football – the most titles achieved by any player in the league’s history. — DVM, GMA Integrated News

headline: Eduard Folayang to battle Aoki for the fourth time at ONE 172  GMA News Online
text:
The fourth chapter of the storied rivalry between mixed martial arts legends Eduard Folayang and Shinya Aoki is set to take centerstage once again when they duke it out at ONE 172 on March 23 at Saitama Super Arena in Japan.
Folayang, 23-13 as a professional MMA fighter, first became the ONE lightweight world champion when he scored a major upset against Aoki by defeating the latter via stoppage in 2016. They fought again twice and Aoki both won by submission in the first round.
Coming off a third-round technical knockout over Amir Khan last September 2023, Folayang is looking to score his second straight victory when he takes on the Japanese MMA legend.Aoki, meanwhile, suffered a first-round submission loss in his grappling match against Cole Abate last December. His last MMA outing, however, was a dominant first-round submission over former world champion John Lineker in January 2024.
—JKC, GMA Integrated News

headline: Naoya Inoue vs Sam Goodman called off anew  GMA News Online
text:
The super bantamweight undisputed showdown between Japanese superstar Naoya Inoue and Sam Goodman has been called off for the second time.
Originally scheduled to square off last month, the bout was moved to January 24 after Goodman absorbed a cut in sparring.
The Australian boxer, however, needed to beg off anew after he suffered a recurring eye injury according to his promoter No Limit Boxing.
"No Limit Boxing regrets to announce that Sam Goodman has been forced out to withdraw from his scheduled bout against Naoya Inoue on January 24 due to a recurrence of his eye injury," No Limit Boxing announced on Instagram.
 
A post shared by No Limit Boxing (@nolimit_aus)
 
"We wish Sam a full and speedy recovery and will provide updates on the event in due course."
Inoue will instead face Ye Joon Kim in the bout.
—JKC, GMA Integrated News

headline: Eumir Marcial, wife Princess open up about their separation  GMA News Online
text:
Filipino Olympic bronze medalist Eumir Marcial and his wife Princess opened up about the status of their relationship.
Princess revealed on Friday that she parted ways with her husband.
In a social media post on Friday, Princess revealed the instances leading to her decision.
"I caught Eumir having an affair in October last year. I, along with my sister, have personally caught them inside an Airbnb condominium in Pasay where we had them both arrested at a police station," Princess wrote in her post, adding that Eumir and the alleged mistress were detained for five days.
Princess said she still gave Eumir several chances after the incident.
"Despite some of my family’s strong disapproval, I still gave Eumir multiple chances to make amends with our marriage, putting into consideration all that we have gone through and the difficult things we have survived as a couple. As his wife, I felt then that I still owe Eumir the responsibility to forgive him so I permitted his release by signing an affidavit of desistance while his mistress signed the compromise agreement thinking Eumir would take this chance to correct his mistake and do better. But what I got instead was him further manipulating me," she added.
Princess also said that according to some of their mutual friends, Eumir and the mistress' relationship still continued.
"Currently, I’ve managed to maintain some correspondence with some of our mutual and trusted friends in Zamboanga City where they alluded that Eumir and his mistress have decided to live together. Apparently, he withdrew all our savings and used the money to construct a house for him and his mistress in Zamboanga. I also have friends who have personally seen them together," she added.
"For these obvious reasons, I had to go on separate ways with Eumir and severed all sources of communication with him after all the trauma, manipulation, emotional stress, physical abuse, and psychological torment that I had to endure these past few months and throughout our years of being together," said Princess.
Princess said she went public because she was concerned with her family's safety and to help other women facing similar situations.
" I don't think I can ever survive the pain, but my hope is that by speaking up, and in a way help other women who may have been suffering in silence, would give me the strength to continue in fighting for what is right in front of God who stood as a witness to our marriage. I’ll leave it up to God’s hands to see that true and righteous justice be served to Eumir and his mistress."
On Saturday, Eumir spoke to GMA Integrated News about Princess' post.
"Ang totoo noon, hindi totoo lahat ng mga sinabi niya sa akin," Eumir said.
"Dahil una-una, kung sinasaktan ko siya, bakit siya magkakaroon ng pasa na ganoon pasa lang? Bakit siya magkaroon ng pasa sa paa? Dahil siya yung nagsasaktan sa akin, pinuprotektahan ko sa sarili ko na huwag niya ako saktan at natatamaan ko siya dahil sa pagprotect ako sa akin sarili at nagkakaroon siya ng pasa. Do you think na dahil sa pananakit ko sa kanya na gusto pa rin niya sa akin mag-stay? Ginagawa niya nito sa akin ngayon dahil ayaw ko na makisama sa kanya dahil sa ugali niya."
Eumir also aired his side on the photo posted by Princess.
"Yung sa likod ng picture na 'yun, hindi talaga picture 'yun, video 'yun. Video 'yun na sinasaktan nila 'yung babae, dalawa sila ng kapatid niya na pilit kong pinuprotektahan. Walang nangyayari na pagtataksil dahil pumunta siya sa akin na hindi niya kami nahabol na nakapatong o may ginagawa ng bagay," he said.
Eumir also said he and Princess were already not on good terms even before this incident.
"Hindi na maayos yung relasyon niya dahil parati kami nag-aaway, parati niya ako sinasaktan. Alam ng mga kaibigan ko 'yan, alam ng pamilya niya, kapatid niya, alam nila lahat yan. Kung dumating kami sa punto ngayon, alam nila na mangyayari ito dahil sa ugali niya."
Eumir also said the woman in the posted photo is his friend.
"Isang beses na pinuntahan ko kasi taga-Zamboanga siya eh. Nagkita kami sa Makati na nagkita kami, taga-Zamboanga pala, nagkakilala, nag-usap. Actually nung nagsama kami may kasama siyang kaibigan, nung nagsama may kasama din ako. Accidentally, nahulog niya 'yung wallet niya sa sasakyan," Eumir narrated.
"Hindi ko inasahan na nandun pala 'yung wallet niya, nahulog, maliit na wallet. Tinago ko, nilagay ko sa bag. Umuwi na siya ng Zamboanga noon eh. Nakita ng asawa ko 'yung wallet niya, doon nagsimula lahat, 'yung paghinala niya, ginawan niya na ng istorya na may babae ako. Kinontak nila 'yung babae, inaway nila. 'Yung babae na kababayan natin dito sa Zamboanga, kinausap sila at gusto kunin 'yung wallet, gusto lang ibalik 'yung wallet. So pumunta ng Maynila para kunin 'yung wallet. Nag-insist ako na tulungan ko siya, puntahan ko siya. Nung time na 'yun, nag-book siya ng hotel sa condo, pinuntahan ko siya doon.
"Inabutan kami ng 12 pm, hapon na. Pinuntahan ko siya, 'yung pala sinusundan na ako kasi may pagdududa na siya kasi hindi na maayos 'yung ano namin. Pinuntahan ko 'yung babae para tulungan siya na kunin 'yung wallet niya. Doon kami inabutan, doon kami na wala namang ginagawa, nag-uusap lang kami. Kung gusto nila malaman, kunin nila 'yung full video kung paano nila sinaktan ang babae. Hindi naman inabot na nakahubad o may ginagawa kami doon. Pumunta ako para tulungan ko siya kunin 'yung wallet eh ganoon ang ginawa nila. Sinaktan nila, binugbog nila ang babae," he said.
Watch the full interview here:
Princess and Eumir got married in 2021 after Marcial bagged the bronze medal in the Tokyo Olympics.
—JKC, GMA Integrated News

headline: PH Consulate visits Pinoys in shelters for LA wildfires victims  GMA News Online
text:
LOS ANGELES, CA - The Philippine Consulate in Los Angeles visited Filipinos at the Pasadena Evacuation Center where victims of the ongoing wildfires are seeking shelter. 
The Consulate said they are also currently coordinating with the Filipino community to assess the situation of those who lost their homes, some of whom have been taken in by fellow Filipino friends.
The Consulate has yet to provide an exact count of Filipino citizens affected.
"Los Angeles authorities have been unable to provide information on the exact number of Philippine citizens affected by the wildfires," said the Public Diplomacy and Information Section of the Philippine Consulate in Los Angeles.
"The Consulate General nevertheless continues to reach out to local authorities and Filipino-American organizations to determine the situation of any affected Philippine citizen and stands ready to provide assistance as allowed under existing laws," it added. 
Previously, Filipinos and Filipino-Americans affected by the wildfires appealed for help after some of them lost everything to the fire. 
 
The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) earlier also said that it is ready to aid Filipinos affected by the massive wildfires. 
“Sa ngayon, we've been trying to reach 'yung ating mga kababayan through all possible means... Marami sa ating mga kababayan ay under mandatory evacuation,” said DFA Assistant Secretary Adelio Angelito Cruz in an interview on Super Radyo dzBB.
Cruz said that so far only three Filipino families have reached out for help from the Philippine government.
“Kakaunti lamang ang humihingi ng tulong sa amin. We are assuming na marami silang kamag-anak sa LA at doon muna sila na naninirahan... Sa Palisades, walang masyadong mga Pilipino,” he said.
(Only a few people have reached out for help. We are assuming that they stayed with their relatives in LA. There are only a few Filipinos in the Palisades.)
The DFA official said there are over three million Filipinos in the entire state of California.
Curfews have been imposed in areas that have burned or are still burning due to incidents of looting.
The National Guard is now assisting the Los Angeles Sheriff’s Department in securing homes under mandatory evacuation orders.
—VAL, GMA Integrated News

headline: DOH debunks rumors of HIV-contaminated blood sugar testing  GMA News Online
text:
The Department of Health (DOH) has dismissed false claims circulating online about supposed "Faculty of Medicine" members visiting households to conduct blood sugar tests using HIV-contaminated needles.
In a statement issued on Saturday, the DOH clarified that the Philippine National Police had already debunked the rumor, labeling it a baseless scare tactic.
“The DOH urges the public not to share unverified claims that may cause unnecessary alarm," the statement read.
The Health Department also encouraged the public to rely solely on legitimate sources and official platforms for accurate information. — DVM, GMA Integrated News

headline: NBA Nuggets get two triple-doubles, thrash Nets  GMA News Online
text:
Nikola Jokic had 35 points, 15 assists and 12 rebounds, Russell Westbrook added 25 points, 11 rebounds and 10 assists, and the host Denver Nuggets beat the Brooklyn Nets 124-105 on Friday.
Jokic recorded his league-leading 15th triple-double of the season and the 145th of his career after missing the previous two games due to an illness. It was the second time this season Jokic and Westbrook recorded triple-doubles in the same game.
Westbrook holds the NBA record with 202, with three of them coming this season.
Michael Porter Jr. scored 17 points, Julian Strawther contributed 12, Christian Braun added 11 points and Peyton Watson had 10 for Denver, which has won six its past eight games.
Nuggets guard Jamal Murray didn't play in the second half due to left knee soreness.
Keon Johnson scored 22 points, Tyrese Martin had 19, Noah Clowney scored 14 and Tosan Evbuomwan, Ben Simmons, Nic Claxton and Ziaire Williams had 10 points each.
Simmons returned to the Brooklyn lineup after missing the prior four games because of calf and back soreness.
The Nuggets led by six at halftime and built on that throughout the third quarter. Jokic scored Denver's first five points of the second half, fed Braun and Porter for 3-pointers, then assisted on Porter's layup and Westbrook's trey that made it 82-64.
The lead grew to 94-73 late in the third period, but the Nets mounted a comeback in the fourth.
Evbuomwan scored eight points in the first 2:27 of the final period to spark a 13-3 run. The lead was 101-95 after Johnson drained a 3-pointer, but the Nuggets started to pull away again.
Westbrook made four free throws, Braun sank two from the line and Braun hit a running dunk in a 16-5 run that made it 117-100, and Denver closed it out.
Brooklyn led by as many as eight points in the first quarter, but the Nuggets used a 16-2 run in the second quarter to lead 56-48. Johnson hit a layup just before the end of the period to get the Nets within 65-59 at halftime.
--Field Level Media/Reuters

headline: PCG continues radio challenge to China Coast Guard vessel off Zambales coast  GMA News Online
text:
The Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) on Friday continued its radio challenge against a China Coast Guard (CCG) vessel off the coast of Zambales in the West Philippine Sea.
The PCG on Saturday said CCG vessel 3304 was illegally sailing at approximately 70-80 nautical miles off the coast of Zambales. During the radio challenge, the PCG warned the vessel that it was within the Philippines' exclusive economic zone (EEZ).
In a statement, the PCG said its vessel BRP Teresa Magbanua (MRRV-9701) remains deployed in the vicinity in compliance with President Ferdinand Marcos Jr.'s directive "aimed at thwarting the normalization and legitimization of the Chinese Coast Guard’s illegal actions, which may lead to assertions of control over these waters."
"Moreover, the continued vigilance of BRP Teresa Magbanua serves as a proactive measure to ensure that Filipino fishermen can carry out their activities without the threat of harassment or intimidation," the PCG said.
"The deployment of the PCG’s white ship underscores a commitment to monitoring and maintaining a visible presence to deter illegal activities by the Chinese Coast Guard while adhering to principles of restraint and non-provocation," the PCG added.
On Saturday, January 4, the PCG confirmed the presence of the so-called ''monster ship'' of the CCG (vessel 5901) some 54 nautical miles away from Capones Island, Zambales.
The PCG then dispatched BRP Cabra, a helicopter, and the PCG Caravan, which confirmed at 5 p.m. that the Chinese vessel was in the area.
It also challenged the presence of the CCG by asserting that the vessel was inside the Philippines' EEZ.
On Wednesday, January 8, the PCG said the monster ship departed from the coastline of Zambales. However, the PCG said it has tracked another CCG vessel (3103) heading directly to the previous location of the monster ship.
On Thursday, January 9, the PCG said the BRP Teresa Magbanua issued radio challenges to CCG vessels that were spotted off the Zambales coast. The PCG noted that CCG vessel 3103 was replaced by vessel 3304 in the afternoon.
China claims "indisputable sovereignty" over the entire waters of the South China Sea, where undersea gas deposits have been discovered in several areas. Its claims overlap with the offshore territories claimed by Asian neighbors surrounding the South China Sea.
In 2016, the Philippines scored a victory  against China in a landmark ruling by the Permanent Court of Arbitration. The PCA ruled that "there was no legal basis for China to claim historic rights to resources within the sea areas falling within the nine-dash line."
"[Although] two Chinese navigators and fishermen, as well as those of other States, had historically made use of the islands in the South China Sea, there was no evidence that China had historically exercised exclusive control over the waters or  their  resources," it added.
China has repeatedly said the PCA ruling is "illegal" and "invalid".
Marcos has stood his ground that the West Philippine Sea belongs to the Filipinos amid China's aggression in the resource-rich region.
West Philippine Sea is the name the Philippines uses to refer to portions of the South China Sea that falls within its exclusive economic zone and its jurisdiction. —KG, GMA Integrated News

headline: PCG China ‘monster ship’ departs Zambales coast  GMA News Online
text:
The China Coast Guard (CCG) vessel 5901, the so-called “monster ship”, has departed from the coastline of Zambales, the Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) said on Wednesday.
“Based on the Philippine Coast Guard's (PCG) current monitoring, the China Coast Guard vessel 5901, ‘monster ship,’ has departed from the coastline of Zambales and is now approximately 90 nautical miles offshore,” the PCG said in a statement.
But the local authorities noted that another vessel from China is nearing Pandaquit, Zambales “likely serving as a replacement vessel” for CCG 5901.
“Meanwhile, the PCG has tracked CCG-3103, which left Guangdong Province yesterday and is heading directly to the previous location of the monster ship,” the Coast Guard added. “As of 3 PM today, CCG-3103 was detected by Canada’s Dark Vessel Detection at a distance of 60 nautical miles from Pandaquit, Zambales.”
While China is “sustaining its illegal presence” within the country’s exclusive economic zone, the PCG deployed an Islander helicopter and the BRP Cabra patrol vessel to the location of CGG-3103 “to monitor and challenge its presence.”
There were nine large CCG vessels, including the 5901 and the 3103, that were spotted in the Philippines’ EEZ in the highly contested West Philippine Sea.
The 165-meter, 12,000-ton "monster ship"—reputed as the largest coast guard ship in the world—was recently spotted 54 nautical miles away from Capones Island, Zambales on Saturday. This was within the 200-nautical mile EEZ of the Philippines.
The “monster ship” on Monday morning moved near Lubang Island in Occidental Mindoro.
The presence of the massive CCG vessel in the country’s EEZ was viewed “with concern” by Malacanang.—Vince Angelo Ferreras/RF, GMA Integrated News

headline: PCG issues radio challenges to latest Chinese vessels off Zambales  GMA News Online
text:
The Philippine Coast Guard’s (PCG) BRP Teresa Magbanua issued radio challenges to China Coast Guard vessels that were spotted off the Zambales coast.
In a statement on Thursday, PCG said Chinese Coast Guard (CCG) vessels were spotted 70 to 80 nautical miles from the shore of Zambales and exhibited "erratic movements."
“In a demonstration of their commitment to protecting the country's maritime sovereignty, the crew of the BRP Teresa Magbanua persistently radio challenged the Chinese vessels, informing them that they are operating within the Philippines' Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ),” PCG said.
“The crew made it clear the CCG vessels do not possess any legal authority to patrol within the Philippines' EEZ and directed them to depart immediately,” it added.
PCG noted that CCG vessel 3103 was replaced by vessel 3304 in the afternoon.
“Despite this change, the objective of the Chinese deployment remains the same: to assert normalization and legitimacy in these waters. The PCG is steadfast in its mission to counteract these efforts and prevent any normalization of illegal deployment of CCG vessels,” the Coast Guard said.
PCG earlier said CCG vessel 5901, the so-called “monster ship,” departed from the Zambales coast after it was spotted in the area last January 4. —Mariel Celine Serquiña/RF, GMA Integrated News
